sections.markdown:
# CHANGE-CLASS and Instance Updates

CLOS lets a *live instance* change its class at runtime. `(change-class instance new-class)` mutates `instance` so that its class is now `new-class`. Slots present in both classes keep their values; slots only in the old class are dropped; slots only in the new class become unbound (and then their `:initform` may be applied via the next hook).

You can customise the transition by specialising `update-instance-for-different-class`:

```lisp
(defmethod update-instance-for-different-class
    :before ((old basic-account) (new premium-account) &key)
  ;; copy or compute fields here
  )
```

This is unusual in mainstream OO and powerful for live-coding situations: you can redefine a class while the system is running and existing instances are *updated in place* the next time they're touched (a related hook, `update-instance-for-redefined-class`, handles that case).

Day to day you won't reach for `change-class` often, but knowing it exists explains why CLOS feels alive.

sections.code: (progn (defclass basic () ((name :initarg :name :accessor name-of))) (defclass premium (basic) ((perks :accessor perks-of :initform :none))) (let ((u (make-instance 'basic :name "Ada"))) (change-class u 'premium) (list (class-name (class-of u)) (name-of u) (perks-of u))))
sections.explain: U was a BASIC; after CHANGE-CLASS it's a PREMIUM. The shared NAME slot is preserved; the new PERKS slot gets its INITFORM.

sections.code: (progn (defclass basic () ((id :initarg :id :accessor id-of))) (defclass advanced (basic) ((tier :initarg :tier :initform :gold :accessor tier-of))) (defmethod update-instance-for-different-class :before ((o basic) (n advanced) &key) (setf (tier-of n) :upgraded)) (let ((u (make-instance 'basic :id 7))) (change-class u 'advanced) (list (id-of u) (tier-of u))))
sections.explain: The :BEFORE method on UPDATE-INSTANCE-FOR-DIFFERENT-CLASS lets us populate the new slot during the transition.

sections.prompt: Define classes DRAFT (slot TITLE, accessor title-of) and PUBLISHED (subclass of DRAFT, slot PUBLISHED-AT, accessor published-at-of, initform :now). Define PROMOTE that takes a DRAFT instance and CHANGE-CLASSes it to PUBLISHED, returning a list of (title published-at).
